perturbation.original_text: 3. No Representation or Warranty. The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Information is experimental in nature and that THE UNIVERSITY MAKES NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY, WHETHER EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED, WITH RESPECT TO THE INFORMATION, INCLUDING ANY REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY AS TO ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR NON- INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY PROPRIETARY RIGHTS.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Assignment. The Recipient will not assign all or part of this Agreement without the prior writter consent of the University. 3. No Representation or Warranty. The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Information is experimental in nature.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'No Representation or Warranty' section to follow after the 'Assignment' section makes it less prominent and harder to find, potentially limiting its effect. By splitting it apart, the full impact of the disclaimer may be missed.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Sale of Goods Act, RSBC 1996, c 410, s 20
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 20 of the Sale of Goods Act (British Columbia) implies conditions and warranties regarding the quality and fitness of goods unless explicitly excluded. Moving the 'No Representation or Warranty' clause to a less conspicuous location might be interpreted as an attempt to obscure the disclaimer, potentially rendering it less effective in negating those implied warranties, as the court might deem the disclaimer not sufficiently brought to the attention of the recipient.

perturbation.justification: The modification creates a legal contradiction because moving the 'No Representation or Warranty' clause to a less prominent location diminishes its effectiveness, potentially conflicting with Section 20 of the Sale of Goods Act (British Columbia), which implies conditions and warranties unless explicitly excluded. The altered text makes it harder for the recipient to be aware of the disclaimer, undermining its ability to negate those implied warranties. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to section 20: 'No waiver of warranties or conditions' which suggests that any attempt to obscure the disclaimer could be viewed as an ineffective waiver, leading to a contradiction of the law because consumers would be unaware of it. The perturbation type, 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction', is fitting because the required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section, making it difficult to interpret, resulting in a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Use. The Recipient will not use the Information for any purpose other than to evaluate the Information for commercial potential. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Recipient will not use the Information to develop, or cause to develop, all or part of any process or product whether for internal use or for commercial purposes. The Recipient hereby indemnifies, holds harmless and defends the University, its Board of Governors, directors, officers, employees, faculty, students and agents against any and all claims, demands, liabilities and expenses (including reasonable legal fees and disbursements), whether direct, indirect, consequential or otherwise, resulting from a breach of this provision or any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Entire Agreement and Counterpart. This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter. 4. Use. The Recipient will not use the Information for any purpose other than to evaluate the Information for commercial potential.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the second half of the 'Use' clause to be immediately following 'Entire Agreement' separates the indemnification clause from the permitted use restrictions. This makes the indemnification less directly linked to misuse of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Canadian contract law principles of contra proferentem.
perturbation.law_explanation: Under the principle of contra proferentem, ambiguities in a contract are interpreted against the party who drafted it. By separating the indemnification clause from the clause defining appropriate use, the link between the two is weakened. If the recipient breaches the use clause and the university seeks indemnification, the court may interpret the weakened connection against the university (as the drafter), potentially limiting the scope of indemnification available to the University. Making it more difficult to enforce the entire clause.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: contra proferentem | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you contra proferentem Contra proferentem is a rule of contract interpretation that states an ambiguous contract term should be construed against the drafter of the contract. The term contra proferentem is derived from a Latin phrase meaning “ against the offeror .” Contra proferentem has become increasingly important with the rise of contracts of adhesion . Contracts of adhesion involve pre-written contracts which are offered on a strict take-it or leave-it basis, leaving no opportunity for a party to bargain over specific contractual terms. Because the party does not have an opportunity to negotiate, they may reasonably interpret a term in a different manner than the contract offeror intended. Contra proferentem exists to place the burden of ambiguity on the party most capable of mitigating that ambiguity - the person who wrote it. The doctrine of contra proferentem is also especially important in the field of insurance law due to the generalized nature of many of its terms. For example, it may be unclear if a policy that covers “water damage” will cover damage caused by a rainstorm induced mudslide. The doctrine of contra proferentem encouraged insurance providers to create enumerated lists of events that are excluded under a given policy, ultimately increasing clarity for insurance purchasers. [Last reviewed in July of 2022 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Keywords contracts Wex COMMERCE commercial activities consumer protection insurance LIFE EVENTS standards of tort liability tort damages financial events THE LEGAL PROCESS courts legal practice/ethics business law contracts wex definitions business sectors commercial transactions individual rights legal education and practice legal theory Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by separating the indemnification clause from the use clause. The Law Explanation states that this weakened connection could be interpreted against the University, limiting the scope of indemnification. This aligns with the principle of contra proferentem, as the scraped law snippet explains: 'an ambiguous contract term should be construed against the drafter of the contract.' By weakening the link between the use clause and indemnification, ambiguity is introduced which could make it more difficult to enforce the entire clause, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Governing Law and Jurisdiction. This Agreement will be governed by and construed under the laws of British Columbia and the applicable laws of Canada without reference to its conflict of law rules. Any action or proceeding brought to enforce the terms of this Agreement will be brought in a court in Vancouver, British Columbia, and the parties hereby consent and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of such court.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Return or Destruction of Information. At the written request of the University or upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return or destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information. 11. Governing Law and Jurisdiction. This Agreement will be governed by and construed under the laws of Canada without reference to its conflict of law rules.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific jurisdiction of Vancouver, British Columbia, the enforceability is weakened. There is no explicit indication that any action or proceeding brought to enforce the terms of this agreement must happen in Vancouver.
perturbation.law_explanation: Removing the location from 11 and placing it at the end removes the parties consent and submission to the exclusive jurisdiction of such court. Rule 14-1 concerns service outside British Columbia. The parties explicitly agreed to the British Columbia court, and removal of that information may have legal consequences regarding service and enforcement, as the defendant might argue lack of jurisdiction. It is crucial to specify where a dispute will be resolved. Without it, it is unclear where a dispute must be brought.

perturbation.justification: The original text specifies Vancouver, British Columbia, as the jurisdiction, while the changed text only mentions Canada. The Law Explanation states that removing this specific location weakens enforceability, as it eliminates the parties' explicit consent and submission to that court's jurisdiction. Rule 14-1 regarding service outside British Columbia could become relevant, and the defendant might argue lack of jurisdiction. The scraped snippets are not relevant to this determination. The Perturbation Explanation supports this, stating the absence of a specific location weakens enforceability. This contradiction is not minor; it directly affects where legal action can be taken, making it harder to enforce the agreement.
